title: System and methodology for selectably partially enclosing articles
abstract: A method for wrapping an article with a circumferential image, the method including providing a sleeve of a heat-shrinkable material, the sleeve bearing a desired circumferential image, arranging the sleeve of a heat-shrinkable material over a circumference of an article and manually applying heat to the heat-shrinkable material, thereby causing it to shrink and tightly engage the article.
claims: Claims 1. A method for wrapping an article with a circumferential image, the method comprising: providing a sleeve of a heat-shrinkable material, said sleeve bearing a desired circumferential image; arranging said sleeve of a heat-shrinkable material over a circumference of an article; and manually applying heat to said heat-shrinkable material, thereby causing it to shrink and tightly engage said article. 2. A method for wrapping an article with a circumferential image according to claim 1 and wherein said step of manually applying heat to said heat shrinkable material comprises placing said article having said sleeve of a heat-shrinkable material arranged over a circumference thereof in a bath of heated water. 3. A method for wrapping an article with a circumferential image according to claim 1 and wherein said step of manually applying heat to said heat shrinkable material comprises: placing said article having said sleeve of a heat-shrinkable material arranged over a circumference thereof in a microwave oven; and operating said microwave oven at an energy level and for a duration suitable for shrinking said sleeve of a heat-shrinkable material into tight engagement with said article. 4. A method for wrapping an article with a circumferential image according to claim 1 and wherein said step of manually applying heat to said heat shrinkable material comprises directing a hair dryer at said article having said sleeve of a heat- shrinkable material arranged over a circumference thereof at an energy level and for a duration suitable for shrinking said sleeve of a heat-shrinkable material into tight engagement with said article. 5. A method for wrapping an article with a circumferential image according to claim 1 and also comprising using a computer to generate said circumferential image. 6. A system for enabling manually wrapping an article with a desired circumferential image comprising: a computer running software which enables a user to generate a desired circumferential image; a printer operative to print said desired circumferential image on a heat- shrinkable substrate; and a printing instruction generator responsive to an input from said computer for causing said printer to print said desired circumferential image on said heat- shrinkable substrate. 7. A system for enabling manually wrapping an article with a desired circumferential image according to claim 6 and also comprising an Internet communication link interconnecting said computer and said printing instruction generator. 8. A system for enabling manually wrapping an article with a desired circumferential image according to claim 6 and wherein said printing instruction generator is imbedded in said software running on said computer.
